evals.prompt: I have a Python tkinter settings panel that's showing up completely blank — no labels, no inputs, nothing. The app launches fine and the main window works. Here's the code: it calls `load_settings()` inside `__init__` before `self.pack()` is ever called on the frame. Can you debug and fix it?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) identify the root cause from the description (pack() order issue), (2) describe a plan to launch the app, screenshot it, and verify the blank panel, (3) implement a fix moving self.pack() before load_settings(), (4) relaunch and screenshot again to confirm the fix.

assertions.name: identifies_root_cause_before_fixing
assertions.description: Agent writes a diagnosis of the root cause before editing any code

assertions.name: uses_screenshot_skill
assertions.description: Agent references or invokes the screenshot-eval skill to visually verify the bug and/or fix

assertions.name: fix_is_minimal
assertions.description: The fix targets only the pack() ordering issue, not a broader refactor

assertions.name: relaunches_after_fix
assertions.description: Agent relaunches the application after applying the fix to verify it worked

evals.prompt: My Electron app has a dropdown menu in the top nav that should show 4 options when clicked, but it's only showing 2. I need to figure out if this is a data issue (the options aren't loaded) or a rendering issue (they're loaded but not visible). The app is running at localhost:3000. Can you help me debug this?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) clarify whether to screenshot a browser or the Electron window, (2) launch/navigate to the app, (3) use a Python script (pyautogui or similar) to click the dropdown to trigger it, (4) screenshot to see the actual state, (5) inspect the relevant component code to distinguish data vs render issue, (6) apply fix and verify.

assertions.name: distinguishes_data_vs_render
assertions.description: Agent explicitly investigates whether the issue is a data problem or a rendering problem

assertions.name: uses_interaction_script
assertions.description: Agent uses a Python automation script or browser interaction to open the dropdown before screenshotting

assertions.name: screenshots_triggered_state
assertions.description: Agent captures a screenshot with the dropdown in its open/triggered state to see what's actually rendered

assertions.name: proposes_concrete_fix
assertions.description: Agent proposes a specific code change (not just 'investigate further')

evals.prompt:
The dark mode toggle in my PyQt5 app doesn't do anything when I click it. The checkbox visually toggles but the window background stays white. I've been staring at the stylesheet code for an hour and can't figure it out. Here's the relevant method:

```python
def toggle_dark_mode(self, checked):
    if checked:
        self.setStyleSheet('QWidget { background-color: #1e1e1e; color: #ffffff; }')
```

The checkbox is connected via `checkbox.stateChanged.connect(self.toggle_dark_mode)`. What's wrong?
evals.expected_output: Agent should: (1) identify that stateChanged passes an int (0/2) not a bool, so `checked` is always truthy when non-zero but the light-mode branch is missing, OR identify a parent widget scoping issue, (2) screenshot the app to confirm the symptom, (3) implement the fix, (4) relaunch and screenshot to verify dark mode activates.

assertions.name: identifies_statechanged_int_issue_or_scoping
assertions.description: Agent correctly identifies either the stateChanged int vs bool issue or a widget scoping problem as the root cause

assertions.name: uses_screenshot_to_verify_symptom
assertions.description: Agent takes a screenshot to confirm the visual symptom (white background persisting)

assertions.name: fix_addresses_both_on_and_off_states
assertions.description: The fix handles both the checked and unchecked states of the toggle

assertions.name: confirms_fix_visually
assertions.description: Agent takes a screenshot after the fix to show dark mode is now applying
